content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected tree having NNN nodes. The color of each node is either Black or White.

In one step, you can choose a node uuu, toggle the color of the chosen node uuu, as well as toggle the colors of all the nodes vvv, such that {u,v}\{u, v\}{u,v} is an edge in the tree.

Can you find out the minimum number of steps required to make the color of all the nodes black?

**Note:** Since the input is large, prefer using fast input methods.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​. AiA_iAi​ = 0 represents that the initial color of the ithi^{th}ith node is black. AiA_iAi​ = 1 represents that the initial color of the ithi^{th}ith node is white.
The following N−1N-1N−1 lines contain two space-separated integers xxx and yyy, which denotes that there is an edge between nodes xxx and yyy.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the minimum number of steps required to make the color of all the nodes black.


Output −1-1−1 if it is impossible to make the color of all the nodes black.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: All the nodes are already black, so we do not need to do anything.
Test Case 222: All the nodes are white. If we choose node 111 in the first step, all the nodes will become black. Hence, only 111 step is required to make all the nodes black.
Test Case 333: Choose node 333 in the first step. This will make all the nodes white. Choose node 111 in the second step. This will make all the nodes black.
